appraisal_events.evidence: « multipliant les mensonges et les tromperies », « la réalité du vice d’Albertine », « elle était partie goûter […] jusqu’à mordre cette petite blanchisseuse […] à qui elle disait : “Tu me mets aux anges.” »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator reconfigures Albertine as an other and “vicieuse,” person, marked by lying and sapphic pleasures, which strongly lowers her in his judgment.

appraisal_events.evidence: « je lui demandai tendrement si l’histoire de la blanchisseuse était vraie. Elle me jura que non […] je lui pardonnai. »
appraisal_events.explanation: After having reconstituted an “Albertine bonne,” the narrator grants her credit and pardon; this about-face softens or locally raises his esteem of Albertine.

status_effects.explanation: She is strongly depreciated by the insistent portrayal of her “vice,” her deceptions, and her otherness.
